hero_subtitle: SFTY connects Travel Rule message data with wallet activity and cases to help fintech teams manage international stablecoin transfer workflows with clear audit evidence and exception handling.

problem_title: Travel Rule workflows slow when data gaps appear
problem_description: Stablecoin issuers face fragmented visibility when originator and beneficiary transfer data is incomplete or scattered across providers. This leads to slower exception handling, weaker audit trails, and challenges in monitoring cross-border transfers with clear compliance evidence.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates originator and beneficiary data, routing status, exceptions, wallets and compliance actions into one workflow, giving fintech teams unified visibility and audit evidence for cross-border stablecoin transfers.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule exceptions to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY tracks cross-border Travel Rule messages through exception handling, links originator and beneficiary data, opens cases, preserves evidence, and supports compliance review workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects Travel Rule messaging, provider outputs, exceptions, cases and evidence into one compliant workflow layer without replacing existing Travel Rule providers.

why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for cross-border stablecoin transfers?
faq_1_answer: SFTY structures Travel Rule data, customer records and stablecoin transfers into one compliance workflow, helping fintechs monitor cross-border flows with audit-ready case management and regulatory rule updates.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle Travel Rule exceptions?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes and escalates exception cases for review, applying internal policies and preserving decision logs to support exceptions are managed consistently within your compliance operations.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve Travel Rule evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY records complete audit evidence including message status, originator and beneficiary data, review actions and case history to support traceability and regulatory inspection readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing Travel Rule providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above existing Travel Rule providers, connecting message outputs into structured workflows without replacing them, enabling seamless orchestration with your current compliance stack.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule messaging providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace messaging providers but complements them by consolidating outputs into a controlled compliance layer with evidence, cases and reporting workflows.
final_title: Connect Travel Rule transfers to controlled workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY structures stablecoin transfer data, exceptions, cases and audit evidence into one compliance operating layer.
